summary.resolution_notes: 7/9 P1 fixed (003-009). P1-001 (in-memory state) deferred to Task 2 (SQLite). P1-002 (end-to-end wiring) deferred to Task 7 (integration checkpoint).

findings.title: Business state (emotion, cooldown) stored only in memory
findings.description: The persona engine stores currentEmotion and lastTriggerTimes in closure-scoped variables. If the Electron app restarts or crashes, all emotion state and trigger cooldown history is lost. CLAUDE.md explicitly forbids 'Business state in memory' and requires persistence to DB. The cooldown state is particularly critical: without persistence, the rest-reminder trigger could fire immediately after restart even if it just fired 5 minutes ago.
findings.suggestion: Persist currentEmotion and lastTriggerTimes to a local SQLite database (e.g., better-sqlite3) or electron-store. Load on startup, write on mutation. This also enables observability of the persona state across sessions.

findings.title: Context engine and persona engine are not connected end-to-end
findings.code_snippet:
const contextEngine = injeca.provide('modules:context-engine', () => setupContextEngine())
const personaEngine = injeca.provide('modules:persona-engine', () => setupPersonaEngine())
findings.description: Both engines are registered via injeca but never connected. setupContextEngine returns { screenshotCapture } and setupPersonaEngine returns { evaluate, getEmotionState }, but no timer, IPC handler, or event listener ever invokes personaEngine.evaluate() with data from contextEngine. There is no periodic activity polling, no screenshot trigger loop, and no IPC channel exposing these services to the renderer. The walking skeleton declares connectivity but does not deliver a working end-to-end data flow from activity observation through trigger evaluation to response generation.
findings.suggestion: Add a periodic timer (e.g., setInterval) in a wiring module that: (1) polls activity events or captures screenshots via contextEngine, (2) builds an ActivityContext via buildActivityContext, (3) maps it to TriggerInput, (4) calls personaEngine.evaluate(input), and (5) sends any response to the renderer via IPC. This completes the vertical slice.

findings.title: Electron desktopCapturer.getSources() called without try/catch
findings.description: desktopCapturer.getSources() is an Electron platform API that can throw on permission denial (macOS screen recording permission), when no display server is available, or on other OS-level failures. This async I/O call has no try/catch protection. An unhandled rejection will propagate up and crash the caller with no diagnostic context.
findings.suggestion: Wrap the desktopCapturer call in try/catch. Log the error with context (e.g., 'Screenshot capture failed') using the project's structured logger, then either re-throw a typed error (e.g., CaptureError) or return a Result type.

findings.title: TriggerResult allows invalid state combinations
findings.description: TriggerResult uses a boolean flag plus string fields that are empty when not triggered. This allows four illegal states: (1) triggered=true with empty triggerId/triggerName, (2) triggered=false with non-empty triggerId/triggerName, (3) triggerId set but triggerName empty, (4) triggerName set but triggerId empty. A discriminated union would make illegal states unrepresentable.

findings.title: No-source condition returns empty Buffer instead of signaling error
findings.description: When desktopCapturer returns no screen sources (e.g., permission denied on macOS, headless environment, or unexpected platform behavior), the provider silently returns an empty Buffer. This converts a real error condition into an invalid state: downstream consumers receive a 0-byte buffer that looks like success but contains no useful data. This matches the forbidden pattern 'catch (e) { return null }' -- converting an error to invalid state.
findings.suggestion: Throw a typed error (e.g., ScreenshotCaptureError) when no primary source is available: if (!primarySource) { throw new Error('No screen sources available from desktopCapturer. Check screen recording permissions.') }

findings.title: buildActivityContext exported but never consumed anywhere
findings.description: buildActivityContext is exported from the context-engine package index but has zero consumers in the entire codebase. No module imports or calls it. Per integration rules, code written but not reachable from any entry point is dead-on-arrival. This function is the critical bridge between raw ActivityEvents and the TriggerInput that persona-engine needs, yet it is never used.
findings.suggestion: Wire buildActivityContext into the app-level integration layer. The periodic evaluation loop should call buildActivityContext(events) to produce an ActivityContext, then map it to TriggerInput for persona-engine.evaluate(). Also add unit tests for this function.

findings.title: VectorStore interface defined but no implementation or consumer exists
findings.description: VectorStore is an interface-only definition with no implementation and no consumer. Per CLAUDE.md rules, 'I'll wire it up later' is a red flag. An interface without a consumer or a contract test is dead code that may drift from actual requirements when implementation time comes.
findings.suggestion: Either (a) remove VectorStore from this PR and add it when there is a real consumer and implementation, or (b) add a minimal implementation with a contract test proving the interface shape.

findings.title: ScreenshotCapture.capture() has no error handling for provider
findings.description: ScreenshotCapture.capture() awaits an external I/O provider with no error handling. This class is the boundary wrapper -- it should catch provider errors and add diagnostic context before letting them propagate. Currently a provider failure produces a raw, context-free stack trace.

findings.title: ScreenshotCapture missing error propagation test
findings.description: ScreenshotCapture.capture() wraps an external provider call but there is no test verifying behavior when the provider throws an error. Error propagation is a critical behavioral path for Imperative Shell boundaries wrapping platform APIs.

findings.title: buildActivityContext has no unit test despite being functional core
findings.description: buildActivityContext is a pure function in the functional core layer that aggregates ActivityEvents into an ActivityContext. CLAUDE.md mandates 100% coverage for the functional core, but there is no test file for this function.
findings.suggestion: Create packages/context-engine/src/__tests__/activity-context.test.ts with tests covering: (1) empty events returns zeroed context, (2) single event returns correct fields, (3) multiple events returns last event fields and correct duration, (4) custom gapThresholdMs is passed through correctly.

findings.title: Disguised TODO: 'Future: replaced by LLM-generated responses'
findings.code_snippet: * Future: replaced by LLM-generated responses.
findings.description: This comment describes planned future work rather than explaining why the current code exists. It functions as an implicit TODO without using the forbidden keyword.
findings.suggestion: Remove the forward-looking statement. If the template-based approach is intentional, document why templates were chosen (e.g., 'Template-based generation avoids LLM dependency during initial integration.') without promising a specific future replacement.

findings.title: transitionEmotion missing test for unknown trigger name
findings.code_snippet: return TRIGGER_EMOTION_MAP[trigger.triggerName] ?? currentState
findings.description: The emotion-state-machine tests do not test the fallback behavior when a trigger fires with an unknown triggerName. The source code uses nullish coalescing (??) to fall back to currentState, but this boundary is unverified.
findings.suggestion: Add a test case verifying that an unknown trigger name preserves the current emotion state.

findings.title: Comment references specific future tech (LanceDB) that will rot
findings.code_snippet: * Walking skeleton: defines the contract for future LanceDB implementation.
findings.description: This comment couples the interface documentation to a specific storage technology (LanceDB) that may or may not be adopted. Interface documentation should describe the contract's purpose, not a planned implementation.
findings.suggestion: Replace with: 'Contract for vector storage operations. Implementations may use LanceDB, pgvector, or any embedding-capable store.'

findings.title: Duration and timestamp fields use raw number type
findings.code_snippet:
timestamp: number
continuousWorkDurationMs: number
lastActivityTimestamp: number
findings.description: Multiple fields use bare `number` for timestamps (milliseconds since epoch) and durations (milliseconds). A caller could accidentally pass seconds instead of milliseconds, or mix up a duration with a timestamp. Branded types would catch unit mismatches at compile time.

findings.title: Log call in persona-engine service missing traceId and structured fields
findings.description: CLAUDE.md observability rules require every log entry to include traceId for request correlation. The log.info call passes a context object but omits traceId.
findings.suggestion: Add a traceId to the log context. For periodic background tasks, generate a correlation ID for each evaluation cycle: const cycleId = crypto.randomUUID()

findings.title: generateResponse missing test for unknown trigger/default fallback
findings.code_snippet: const message = templates?.[emotion] ?? templates?.neutral ?? DEFAULT_MESSAGE
findings.description: The response-generator tests only cover the 'rest-reminder' trigger. There is no test for an unknown triggerName that falls through to DEFAULT_MESSAGE, or a known triggerName with an emotion state that has no template. The three-level fallback chain is only partially tested.
findings.suggestion: Add tests for the fallback chain covering unknown trigger names and missing emotion-specific templates.

findings.title: Optional chaining silently falls through on unrecognized trigger
findings.code_snippet:
const templates = RESPONSE_TEMPLATES[trigger.triggerName]
const message = templates?.[emotion] ?? templates?.neutral ?? DEFAULT_MESSAGE
findings.description: When trigger.triggerName is not found in RESPONSE_TEMPLATES, the chain silently falls through to DEFAULT_MESSAGE with no logging or warning. This also silently hides the case where a new trigger is added but its templates are missing -- a programmer error.
findings.suggestion: Add a warning log when templates are not found for a trigger name to preserve fallback behavior while making missing templates observable.

findings.title: calculateContinuousWorkDuration no test for unsorted input
findings.description: The function's JSDoc states events must be sorted ascending by timestamp, but there is no defensive guard and no test documenting what happens with unsorted input.
findings.suggestion: Either add a defensive sort at the start of the function, or add a test that explicitly documents the precondition.

findings.title: TriggerCondition.id uses unbranded string
findings.description: TriggerCondition.id is typed as bare `string` but has an implicit format (e.g., 'T03'). The same `string` type is used for triggerId in TriggerResult and ProactiveResponse, enabling accidental use of triggerName where triggerId is expected.
findings.suggestion: Introduce branded types: type TriggerId = string & { readonly __brand: 'TriggerId' }

findings.title: Walking skeleton scope comment will rot as triggers are added
findings.code_snippet: * Walking skeleton: only T03 (rest-reminder) is mapped.
findings.description: This comment is tightly coupled to the current implementation scope. As soon as additional triggers are mapped, this comment becomes stale and misleading.
findings.suggestion: Remove the scope annotation. Replace with: 'Maps trigger names to the emotion states they induce.'

findings.title: Walking skeleton scope comment will rot on response-generator
findings.code_snippet: * Walking skeleton: only T03 (rest-reminder) templates are defined.
findings.description: Comment states 'only T03 templates are defined' which will become false as soon as another trigger's templates are added.
findings.suggestion: Remove the scope annotation. Replace with: 'Template-based response messages keyed by trigger name and emotion state.'

findings.title: Duplicated gap threshold default across two functions
findings.code_snippet: gapThresholdMs: number = 5 * 60 * 1000,
findings.description: The default gap threshold value `5 * 60 * 1000` is duplicated as a default parameter in both calculateContinuousWorkDuration and buildActivityContext. Since buildActivityContext delegates to calculateContinuousWorkDuration, the default is effectively defined twice.
findings.suggestion: Extract to a shared constant: export const DEFAULT_GAP_THRESHOLD_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000

findings.title: Walking skeleton scope comment on setupContextEngine will rot
findings.code_snippet: * Walking skeleton: only initializes screenshot capture.
findings.description: Comment states 'only initializes screenshot capture' which will become stale when additional context sources are wired in.
findings.suggestion: Replace with: 'Initialize the ContextEngine service for the main process, wiring platform-specific providers.'

findings.title: Walking skeleton scope comment on setupPersonaEngine will rot
findings.code_snippet: * Walking skeleton: only T03 rest-reminder trigger is active.
findings.description: Comment states 'only T03 rest-reminder trigger is active' which will become false as soon as additional triggers are registered.
findings.suggestion: Replace with: 'Initialize the PersonaEngine service for the main process, registering proactive triggers and managing emotion state.'

findings.title: evaluateTriggers missing multi-trigger priority test
findings.code_snippet:
const triggers = [T03_REST_REMINDER]

it('returns triggered=true when a condition is met and not in cooldown', () => {
findings.description: The evaluateTriggers tests only use a single trigger. The function iterates over an array and returns the first match, establishing implicit priority based on array order, but this is untested.
findings.suggestion: When a second trigger is added, include a test verifying first-match priority.

findings.title: ScreenshotCapture class wraps a single method with no state
findings.description: The class holds a single provider field and exposes a single capture() method. This could be a plain function, aligning with the functional style used elsewhere. However, the class may be intentional for future extensibility.
findings.suggestion: Consider replacing with a plain function if no lifecycle hooks or caching are planned. Keep the class if extensibility is intended.

findings.title: VectorStore metadata uses Record<string, unknown>
findings.code_snippet:
store(embedding: number[], metadata: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<string>
search(query: number[], topK: number): Promise<Array<{ id: string, score: number, metadata: Record<string, unknown> }>>
findings.description: The VectorStore interface uses Record<string, unknown> for metadata, providing no type safety for what metadata is stored or retrieved.
findings.suggestion: When implementing beyond the walking skeleton, make VectorStore generic: interface VectorStore<M extends Record<string, unknown>>

positive_observations: Clean Functional Core / Imperative Shell separation: domain logic is implemented as pure functions with no side effects, while platform-specific code is isolated in the shell layer.
Well-designed ScreenshotProvider interface enables dependency inversion: the context-engine package depends on an abstraction, not the Electron API directly.
persona-engine functional core has thorough unit tests with good edge case coverage: cooldown periods, fullscreen suppression, emotion transitions from all states, and an end-to-end integration test.
TriggerInput is intentionally decoupled from ActivityContext, keeping persona-engine independent of context-engine at the type level.
All pure functions accept explicit parameters (including 'now' timestamp) rather than calling Date.now() internally, making them fully deterministic and testable.
Test doubles for external APIs include proper rationale comments as required by CLAUDE.md.
Functional Core modules tested with direct instantiation and pure input/output -- no mocks on domain logic.
End-to-end pipeline test in persona-engine.test.ts validates the full trigger->emotion->response flow using real module imports.
Boundary conditions are well-covered: empty arrays, single elements, exact boundary values, cooldown edge cases.
No forbidden test patterns detected: no jest.fn(), no jest.mock(), no InMemory/Mock/Fake repository classes.
EmotionState uses a string literal union type, making invalid emotion values unrepresentable at compile time.
All interfaces are well-documented with JSDoc comments explaining purpose and field semantics.
No forbidden comment patterns (TODO, FIXME, HACK, XXX) found in any of the 11 files.
JSDoc parameter descriptions accurately match actual function signatures across all files.
All functions have low cyclomatic complexity (1-4 decision points), maximum nesting depth is 2 levels.
Every function is short (under 25 lines), with clean early returns and guard clauses throughout.
Named constants used consistently instead of magic numbers (TWO_HOURS_MS, COOLDOWN_MS, DEFAULT_MESSAGE, INITIAL_EMOTION).
No nested ternaries, no complex conditionals, no deep callback chains.
